concerns.text: FR-008 mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping 'absolute diff ∈ {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}' around the 75th percentile. This is mathematically ambiguous and unverifiable: is the diff applied to the percentile value (e.g., 0.75 ± 0.01) or to the threshold index? Without defining the unit (probability vs. amplitude) or the baseline, a test cannot deterministically verify if the correct thresholds were used.

The Edge Cases section contains a massive, contradictory block of text regarding dataset selection (HCP vs. UK Biobank vs. OpenNeuro vs. COBRE) with multiple 'MUST' statements that cannot all be true simultaneously. It ends with a '[DEFERRED]' marker but fails to define a deterministic fallback logic or a specific 'primary' dataset to test against. A test cannot verify 'switching' logic if the trigger conditions and target datasets are not explicitly defined.

FR-004 states: 'detect neural avalanches by thresholding EEG channels at 75th percentile amplitude'. This is unverifiable without specifying the scope of the percentile calculation (e.g., per-channel, per-subject, or global across all channels/time). Different scopes yield different binary rasters and different avalanche statistics, making the requirement ambiguous.

SC-006 sets a success criterion of 'total runtime ≤ 6 hours on GitHub Actions free‑tier runner'. This is not a functional requirement of the system itself but a constraint on the CI environment. While measurable, it is not a verifiable property of the *spec* without an explicit definition of the 'load profile' (e.g., 'for n=50 participants'). Running on a different dataset size would invalidate the test, making the criterion incomplete.
